task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed the CAMEO Chemicals website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window should be visible showing the CAMEO Chemicals website (cameochemicals.noaa.gov) being used to search for the chemicals.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent created the required text file at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal should show the file '/home/ga/Documents/distillation_schedule.txt' being created or edited.

task_completion.description: Verify the report contains the exact required header.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file must contain the exact header 'DISTILLATION RECOVERY SCHEDULE' followed by '================================' on the next line.

task_completion.description: Verify all five chemicals are listed with their boiling points converted to Celsius.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file should list Acetone, Toluene, Methyl Ethyl Ketone (or 2-Butanone), Isopropyl Alcohol (or 2-Propanol), and Xylene, each with a boiling point in Celsius (C) next to it.

task_completion.description: Verify the chemicals are ordered correctly from lowest to highest boiling point.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The numbered list must be in this exact order: 1. Acetone, 2. Methyl Ethyl Ketone (2-Butanone), 3. Isopropyl Alcohol (2-Propanol), 4. Toluene, 5. Xylene.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent identified the correct critical pair.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file must explicitly state or identify that 'Methyl Ethyl Ketone' (or 2-Butanone) and 'Isopropyl Alcohol' (or 2-Propanol) form the critical pair.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used the web browser to research the chemicals on CAMEO Chemicals.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the CAMEO Chemicals search interface and viewing chemical data pages, rather than just relying on its internal knowledge.

integrity.description: Verify the boiling points recorded are genuine and not hallucinated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The boiling points in the text file should closely match the verified values (Acetone ~56C, MEK ~80C, IPA ~82C, Toluene ~111C, Xylene ~139C) within a 5-degree tolerance.

integrity.description: Verify the output was actually saved to the specified file path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen saving the file in a text editor to '/home/ga/Documents/distillation_schedule.txt' or using a command that writes to this specific path, rather than just printing the text to the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct boiling points (in Celsius) are approximately: Acetone (56.1 C), Methyl Ethyl Ketone / 2-Butanone (80.0 C), Isopropyl Alcohol / 2-Propanol (82.35 C), Toluene (110.6 C), and Xylene (139.0 C). A tolerance of +/- 5 C is acceptable. The correct distillation recovery order (lowest to highest) is: 1. Acetone, 2. Methyl Ethyl Ketone, 3. Isopropyl Alcohol, 4. Toluene, 5. Xylene. The critical pair (the two adjacent chemicals with the smallest temperature difference) is Methyl Ethyl Ketone and Isopropyl Alcohol, with a delta of approximately 2 C.
